title: Sanctuary Falls
tagline: Sanctuary Falls — wildlife reserve of Nagaland

overview.short: The Sanctuary Falls are a waterfall located in Khonoma in the Indian state of Nagaland.
overview.description: The Sanctuary Falls are a waterfall located in Khonoma in the Indian state of Nagaland. It lies within the Khonoma Nature Conservation and Tragopan Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Khonoma Nature Conservation and Tragopan Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Khonoma Nature Conservation and Tragopan Sanctuary or KNCTS is a conservation reserve and a protected area in the Kohima district, Nagaland, India. The total area notified under this park is around 25 square kilometres (9.7 sq mi); some of villages and hamlets are adjacent to this park: Khonoma, Mezoma and Dzüleke.

image.alt: Khonoma Nature Conservation and Tragopan Sanctuary

topPlaces.name: Mount Japfü
topPlaces.description: Mount Japfü (, YAP-FU) is a mountain peak of the Barail Range, located in Kohima District of Nagaland in India, about 15 kilometres (9 mi) south of Kohima, the capital of Nagaland.

topPlaces.name: Dzüleke
topPlaces.description: Dzüleke is a village in Kohima District of the Indian state of Nagaland. The total population of the village is about 156 with 35 households.

topPlaces.name: Khonoma
topPlaces.description: Khonoma is a Western Angami Naga village located about 20 km west from Kohima, the capital of the Indian state of Nagaland. The village is referred to as Khwüno-ra (named after the Angami term for a local plant, Glouthera fragrantissima).

topPlaces.name: Dzukou Valley
topPlaces.description: The Dzukou Valley (also spelt Dzüko, Dzükou or Dzukuo) is a high-altitude valley in Northeast India, straddling the inter-state border of the states of Nagaland and Manipur. Lying behind Mount Japfü at an elevation between 2,438 and 2,452 m (7,999 and 8,045 ft) above sea level in the Kohima and Senapati districts, it is known for its rolling meadows, seasonal wildflowers and rich biodiversity, and is among the most popular trekking destinations in northeastern India.

topPlaces.name: Pulie Badze
topPlaces.description: Pulie Badze is a mountain peak in Kohima District of the Indian state of Nagaland, standing at an elevation of 2,296 m (7,533 ft) overlooking the capital city of Nagaland, Kohima. It is the centerpiece of Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Kezol-tsa Forest
topPlaces.description: The Kezol-tsa Forest is a thick old-growth forest along the southern and northern edges of the Indian states of Nagaland and Manipur. The forest is located on the southern part of Dzüko Valley.

topPlaces.name: Mount Tempü
topPlaces.description: Mount Tempü is a peak of the Barail Range rising at the mountainous border of the Indian states of Manipur and Nagaland. With a height of 2994 m above sea level, is the highest peak of Manipur and the second highest peak in the Barail Range.

items.activity: Khonoma Nature Conservation and Tragopan Sanctuary
items.note: Khonoma Nature Conservation and Tragopan Sanctuary or KNCTS is a conservation reserve and a protected area in the Kohima district, Nagaland, India. The total area notified under this park is around 25 square kilometres (9.7 sq mi); some of villages and hamlets are adjacent to this park: Khonoma, Mezoma and Dzüleke.
Mount Japfü (, YAP-FU) is a mountain peak of the Barail Range, located in Kohima District of Nagaland in India, about 15 kilometres (9 mi) south of Kohima, the capital of Nagaland.
Dzüleke is a village in Kohima District of the Indian state of Nagaland. The total population of the village is about 156 with 35 households.

Khonoma is a Western Angami Naga village located about 20 km west from Kohima, the capital of the Indian state of Nagaland. The village is referred to as Khwüno-ra (named after the Angami term for a local plant, Glouthera fragrantissima).

The Dzukou Valley (also spelt Dzüko, Dzükou or Dzukuo) is a high-altitude valley in Northeast India, straddling the inter-state border of the states of Nagaland and Manipur. Lying behind Mount Japfü at an elevation between 2,438 and 2,452 m (7,999 and 8,045 ft) above sea level in the Kohima and Senapati districts, it is known for its rolling meadows, seasonal wildflowers and rich biodiversity, and is among the most popular trekking destinations in northeastern India.

Pulie Badze is a mountain peak in Kohima District of the Indian state of Nagaland, standing at an elevation of 2,296 m (7,533 ft) overlooking the capital city of Nagaland, Kohima. It is the centerpiece of Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary.
The Kezol-tsa Forest is a thick old-growth forest along the southern and northern edges of the Indian states of Nagaland and Manipur. The forest is located on the southern part of Dzüko Valley.

Mount Tempü is a peak of the Barail Range rising at the mountainous border of the Indian states of Manipur and Nagaland. With a height of 2994 m above sea level, is the highest peak of Manipur and the second highest peak in the Barail Range.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sanctuary Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sanctuary Falls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sanctuary Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dimapur Airport (41 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (46 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sanctuary Falls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sanctuary Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sanctuary Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Sanctuary Falls start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sanctuary Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Khonoma Nature Conservation and Tragopan Sanctuary, Mount Japfü, Dzüleke, Khonoma, Dzukou Valley.
